text: AI patient-specific digital twins (2023-2026): computational models of individual patients created from multi-modal data (imaging, genomics, EHR, wearables) that simulate disease progression and treatment response. Applications: (1) Cardiac digital twins -- Simulia Living Heart (Dassault, 2014-2025) models individual heart electrophysiology, used clinically for arrhythmia ablation planning at Johns Hopkins; (2) Orthopedic twins -- personalized joint models for surgical planning and implant sizing.

AI-driven in silico clinical trials: using virtual patient populations (digital twin cohorts) to simulate drug efficacy and safety, reducing the need for human subjects. Certara, Insilico Medicine, and Novadiscovery deploy AI clinical trial simulation. The FDA (2024) released guidance on computer modeling for medical device submissions, and the EMA (2025) endorsed in silico evidence for drug development, signaling regulatory acceptance.
